content:
# Cafe Floor Plan Template

**Tool ID:** T089-01
**Block:** B089 (Workflow Layout Audit)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Template
**Role:** Owner
**Time Required:** 30-45 minutes (first time), 15 minutes (updates)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. Before you can fix your workflow, you need to see it. This template turns your cafe's physical space into a visible, analysable map.

A ready-to-use template that removes the guesswork. Fill it in, adapt it to your reality, and put it to work.

**Built for Owner working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Graph paper or digital template for drawing the cafe's physical layout showing all equipment, counters, services, and movement paths. Includes a legend for role-specific path colours and annotation symbols. This becomes the base map for all workflow analysis.

This tool supports **Workflow Layout Audit** (B089) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Owner (initial creation), Manager (updates after changes)
**Time Required:** 30-45 minutes for initial drawing; 15 minutes for updates
**Frequency:** Create once. Update whenever the layout changes (new equipment, furniture rearrangement, renovation).

**What You Will Need:**
- This template (printed on A3 for best results)
- Tape measure
- Pen, pencil, and 4 coloured markers (red, blue, green, black)
- Your cafe keys (measure after hours for accuracy)
- Graph paper or grid paper if drawing by hand

**Steps:**
1. Measure the overall dimensions of your cafe (length x width in metres)
2. Choose a scale (recommended: 1 square = 0.5 metres)
3. Draw the walls, doors, and windows first
4. Add fixed elements: counter, plumbing points, electrical outlets
5. Add equipment using the legend symbols below
6. Add moveable elements: tables, chairs, display units
7. Draw movement paths using the colour-coded system
8. Mark bottleneck points and collision zones with a star symbol

---

## Template

### Floor Plan Information

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Cafe name | |
| Address | |
| Date drawn | |
| Drawn by | |
| Total floor area | _____ m x _____ m = _____ sq m |
| Scale used | 1 square = _____ m |
| Version | (increment after each update) |

### Equipment Legend

*Use these symbols consistently on your floor plan:*

| Symbol | Equipment | Typical Dimensions |
|--------|-----------|--------------------|
| [EM] | Espresso machine | 70 x 55 cm |
| [GR] | Grinder (each) | 20 x 35 cm |
| [FR] | Fridge (under-counter or upright) | 60 x 60 cm / 60 x 70 cm |
| [FZ] | Freezer | 60 x 70 cm |
| [DC] | Display case / cake fridge | 90-120 x 70 cm |
| [DW] | Dishwasher / glasswasher | 60 x 60 cm |
| [SK] | Sink (prep / hand-wash) | 40 x 50 cm |
| [OV] | Oven / toaster | varies |
| [BL] | Blender station | 30 x 30 cm |
| [POS] | POS / till point | 40 x 40 cm |
| [KB] | Knock box | 20 x 30 cm |
| [WB] | Waste bin | 30 x 30 cm |
| [HW] | Handwash sink | 30 x 40 cm |

### Movement Path Colour Code

| Colour | Role | Typical Path |
|--------|------|-------------|
| RED | Barista | Grinder > machine > milk fridge > handoff point |
| BLUE | Floor / service staff | POS > tables > clearing station > wash-up |
| GREEN | Kitchen / food prep | Prep area > fridge > oven > display case > pass |
| BLACK | Customer | Entrance > queue > POS > pickup > seating > exit |

### Annotation Symbols

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| Star (*) | Bottleneck / collision point |
| Arrow (>) | One-way flow direction |
| X | Blocked path / dead end |
| Circle (O) | Waiting point (staff or customer) |
| # | Power outlet |
| ~ | Water / plumbing point |

### Floor Plan Grid

*Draw your floor plan in the space below, or use a separate sheet of graph paper at A3 size. Include all walls, doors, windows, fixed equipment, moveable furniture, and movement paths.*

*Recommended approach:*
1. Outline the perimeter walls first (use a ruler)
2. Mark door positions with a gap in the wall line
3. Mark windows with a dashed line
4. Place all fixed equipment using legend symbols
5. Add counter/bar position
6. Place tables and chairs
7. Draw movement paths with coloured lines
8. Mark bottleneck zones with stars

### Layout Analysis Notes

| Zone | Equipment / Furniture | Movement Issues Observed | Improvement Ideas |
|------|-----------------------|-------------------------|-------------------|
| Espresso Bar | | | |
| POS / Till | | | |
| Food Display / Pass | | | |
| Customer Queue | | | |
| Kitchen / Prep | | | |
| Storage | | | |
| Customer Seating | | | |
| Entrance / Exit | | | |
| Toilets | | | |
| Wash-up | | | |

### Key Measurements

| Measurement | Value | Minimum Standard | Pass / Fail |
|-------------|-------|-----------------|-------------|
| Main customer pathway width | _____ cm | 90 cm | P / F |
| Behind-counter working width | _____ cm | 90 cm (120 cm ideal) | P / F |
| Distance: grinder to machine | _____ cm | under 60 cm ideal | P / F |
| Distance: machine to milk fridge | _____ cm | under 80 cm ideal | P / F |
| Distance: POS to drink handoff | _____ cm | under 150 cm ideal | P / F |
| Kitchen / prep area width | _____ cm | 120 cm minimum | P / F |
| Space between table edges | _____ cm | 90 cm minimum (accessibility) | P / F |

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Owner | | | |
| Manager | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Drawing Idealised Layout Instead of Actual

**Why it happens:** Owner draws how the cafe SHOULD look, not how it actually is right now. Equipment that has been moved, tables added for extra covers, or storage creep are not shown.
**How to prevent:** Draw on-site with a tape measure. Walk the space as you draw. Include everything, even temporary items and clutter.
**If it is already happening:** Go to the cafe now and compare your drawing to reality. Mark every difference in red.

### Risk 2: Floor Plan Never Updated After Changes

**Why it happens:** New display case added, tables rearranged for an event and never moved back, grinder repositioned. The floor plan shows last year's layout.
**How to prevent:** Version the floor plan. Any time equipment moves, update the plan and increment the version number. Store old versions for comparison.
**If it is already happening:** Do a fresh walk-through with this template. Treat it as a new baseline.

### Risk 3: Movement Paths Not Traced During Actual Service

**Why it happens:** Paths are drawn based on assumption ("the barista goes from here to here"). But during a real rush, the actual paths involve detours, collisions, and waiting that are not captured.
**How to prevent:** Use T089-02 (Workflow Observation Sheet) to trace ACTUAL paths during a rush. Overlay the observations on this floor plan.
**If it is already happening:** Schedule a 30-minute observation during the next morning rush. Have the Manager trace one barista's movement on the floor plan with a red marker.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Include your pour-over / filter bar as a separate station. Map the customer journey for dine-in vs takeaway separately.
**Chain / Franchise:** Compare your floor plan to the brand standard layout. Identify deviations and whether they help or hinder.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Adapt the grid for your smaller footprint. Every centimetre matters more. Include vehicle/stall dimensions.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add bakery-specific zones: proving area, baking station, decorating area, bread display.

**Making it yours:**
- Use A3 paper for the best detail
- Take a photo of the floor plan and store digitally as backup
- Update after every equipment change, renovation, or furniture move
- Use this as the base layer for workflow observations (T089-02) and improvement plans (T089-04)

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T089-02 -- Workflow Observation Sheet (L1, template)
- T089-03 -- Layout Issue Prioritisation Matrix (L2, template)
- T089-04 -- Layout Improvement Plan (L2, template)
- T089-05 -- Annual Layout Audit Report (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**
- B090

**Parent block:** B089 -- Workflow Layout Audit
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
